article_title: Obligations of providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall ensure an adequate level of cybersecurity protection for the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk and the physical infrastructure of the model.
scoring_text: Knowledge of cybersecurity requirements for GPAI models with systemic risk, including model security architecture, physical infrastructure protection, and adequate cybersecurity level determination.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of cybersecurity requirements for GPAI models with systemic risk, including model security architecture, physical infrastructure protection, and adequate cybersecurity level determination.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of model risk management regulatory expectations, including SR 11-7, SS1/23, and EU AI Act requirements as they apply to AI and ML models in regulated industries, including effective challenge, validation, and ongoing monitoring obligations.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 55(1)(d) (obligation) superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-K-009 is the strongest match (STS: 0.607, cosine: 0.573). Secondary: AG-K-003 (AG, cosine: 0.533), SP-A-002 (SP, cosine: 0.526).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.607). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, model, models, requirements, risk]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [adequate, architecture, cybersecurity, determination, gpai, infrastructure] vs WIDAI terms [11, 23, 7, act, ai, apply] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.881, R=0.856). P exceeds R by 0.026 — the competency implication's 22 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 35 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.573 ≈ STS 0.607 (gap 0.034). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge, model, models, requirements, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, model, models, requirements, risk]. EU AI Act-only: [adequate, architecture, cybersecurity, determination, gpai, infrastructure]. WIDAI-only: [11, 23, 7, act, ai, apply].
